primary_complaint: Bilateral knee pain, right worse than left, x 3+ years
functional_limitations: Cannot use stairs without hand rail
Pain limits ability to demonstrate drills as volunteer coach
objective_measurements: Right knee flexion limited to 95 degrees

imaging_findings.key_findings: X-ray (02/15/2025): Right knee: Severe tricompartmental osteoarthritis with bone-on-bone medial compartment involvement, Kellgren-Lawrence Grade IV, varus deformity 8 degrees. Left knee: Moderate OA, KL Grade III, less severe than right.

surgical_approach_if_mentioned: Anterior approach. Will perform standard primary TKA.

denial_risk_flags.label: Cortisone Injection Dates Undocumented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The two corticosteroid injections are dated only as 'approx 2023, exact date unclear,' which does not meet payer documentation standards requiring specific dates for each injection administered.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Obtain and document exact dates for both cortisone injections from the administering provider's records or pharmacy records before submission.

denial_risk_flags.label: Physical Therapy Duration Below Payer Threshold
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Only 6 weeks of physical therapy is documented. Aetna PPO typically requires a minimum of 6–8 weeks of formal PT with documented failure; 6 weeks is at the lower boundary and may be challenged if outcomes documentation is sparse.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add a chart addendum specifying the number of PT sessions attended, the specific modalities used, and objective functional outcome measures at discharge to strengthen the failure documentation.
denial_risk_flags.anchorText: PT at Lakeside Rehab for 6 weeks

denial_risk_flags.label: No MRI Documented for Surgical Planning
denial_risk_flags.explanation: No MRI of the right knee is documented. While X-ray findings are strong (KL Grade IV, bone-on-bone), some Aetna PPO policies require MRI to rule out soft tissue pathology or confirm surgical candidacy prior to TKA authorization.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Verify whether Aetna PPO's current TKA policy requires MRI; if so, order and document MRI results prior to submission.

denial_risk_flags.label: Elevated BMI May Require Additional Documentation
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Patient BMI is 34.2. Some payers require documentation of weight management counseling or a formal weight loss attempt prior to elective TKA when BMI exceeds 33–35.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add a chart note documenting that BMI-related surgical risk was discussed with the patient and that weight management counseling was offered or completed.

denial_risk_flags.label: Hyaluronic Acid Injection Duration Not Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The Synvisc-One injection dated 06/2024 has no documented duration of treatment effect or follow-up period, making it difficult to demonstrate adequate trial and failure of this modality.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add a follow-up note documenting the patient's response at 4–12 weeks post-injection and confirming failure of the hyaluronic acid injection prior to surgical referral.
denial_risk_flags.anchorText: Synvisc-One hyaluronic acid injection right knee 06/2024

denial_risk_flags.label: Requesting Provider Discrepancy
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The OR field lists Dr. Sandra Reyes MD as the operating surgeon, while Dr. Sandra Reyes is listed as the requesting provider. Payers may flag a mismatch between the operating surgeon and the authorizing provider.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Clarify and reconcile the operating surgeon and requesting provider fields to ensure consistency across all authorization documents submitted to Aetna PPO.

diagnosis_codes.note: Two ICD-10 codes are documented (M17.11, M17.12) with clear laterality and specificity.

conservative_treatments_named.note: Four distinct conservative treatments are named: physical therapy, corticosteroid injections, hyaluronic acid injection, and OTC analgesics.

conservative_treatment_duration.note: Two of four treatments (cortisone injections and Synvisc-One) lack documented duration of the treatment trial, weakening the failure narrative.

imaging_findings.note: X-ray findings are strong with KL Grade IV, bone-on-bone medial compartment, and varus deformity documented with a specific date.

functional_limitations.note: Three specific functional limitations are documented: walking distance, stair use, and occupational/volunteer activity restriction.

surgical_approach.note: Anterior approach is explicitly stated in the operative plan.

cpt_code_valid.note: CPT 27447 is explicitly documented and matches the requested procedure (right total knee arthroplasty).

symptom_duration.note: Symptom duration is explicitly stated as 3+ years.
